raw_narrative: SAN FRANCISCO (SFO), UAL1871, B738. UAL1871 was pushed onto the ramp from Gate D16. The pilot reported to ground control that they may have pushed into another aircraft. Airfield ops and the fire department were called to the scene and confirmed that they had pushed back into an aircraft in Gate E5, UAL769, which was proposed to depart but had not yet pushed from the gate. No injuries were reported; however, the aircraft were pulled back into the gate, and both aircraft deboarded. It was reported to the tower from airfield ops that UAL1871 had a bent horizontal stabilizer and that UAL769 had damaged the exhaust column. FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: Unknown, Injuries: None, Damage: Substantial. 9/2/2025 0412Z.

sources_json: [{"title": "Aviation Safety Network entry for N6831A (Cessna 172 Skyhawk near Anacortes)", "url": "https://aviation-safety.net/wikibase/546506", "publisher": "Aviation Safety Network", "date": "2025-09-22"}]
raw_narrative: ANACORTES, WA, (ZSE), (74S), N6831A, C172. N6831A had sunk in the Guemes Channel. The event was reported by the Skagit County Sheriff's Deputy. The deputy reported there was one survivor picked up by a ferry and no fatalities. The aircraft departed from 74S for FHR (Friday Harbor). The location of the aircraft was N48.31071 W122.67769. FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 1, Injuries: Unknown, Damage: Destroyed. 9/22/2025 1850Z.

CHICO, CA, (CIC), N6991F, C172. N6991F experienced a blown tire while landing on RWY31R. The airport operations was able to tow the aircraft to parking without any issues. FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 1, Injuries: None, Damage: Unknown. 9/22/2025 2040Z.

PORTLAND, OR, (HIO), N59463; A75N1(PT17)STEERMAN. N59463 caught a tailwind, causing it to bounce on landing on RWY 31L and veer hard right across multiple TWYs and RWY20. The aircraft was removed from TWY Delta and stowed in the hangar. FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 2, Injuries: None, Damage: Unknown. 9/22/2025 1945Z.

BELUGA, AK, (WSC ROC), (BLG), N4033Z, PA18. N4033Z was moving from one tie-down spot to another near Beluga, AK, at Theodore River Road. The aircraft ended up in brush and inverted. No FAA Form 8020-9 was received. UPDATE (9/24/2025): FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 1, Injuries: None, Damage: Unknown. Late Report: 9/15/2025 1800Z.

KUSKOKWIM, AK, (ENA FSS), N4743T, M4. N4743T crashed near the south fork of the Kuskokwim River at 61.71947N/ -153.36714W. FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 3, Injuries: None, Damage: Substantial. Late Report: 9/19/2025 0535Z.

FALLBROOK, CA, (WSC ROC), N766EE, C182. N766EE’s engine failed while landing on RWY18 at L18 into the Los Jilgueros Preserve. The aircraft ended up in an embankment. No FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 1, Injuries: None, Damage: Substantial. 9/23/2025 2105Z.

LAKE HAVASU CITY, AZ, (FSOC FSS), (HII), N462HP, PA46. N462HP failed to lock gear down while landing on RWY32, causing the gear to collapse. FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 1, Injuries: None, Damage: Unknown. 9/23/2025 1600Z.

FAIRBANKS, AK, (FAI FSS), (FAI), N976E, C208. N976E was taking off from an unidentified mining strip when the left wing made contact with a front-end loader parked off to the side of the runway. FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 4, Injuries: None, Damage: Unknown. 9/22/2025 0030Z.

raw_narrative: EASTON, WA, (ZSE), N955AL, EC135. N955AL was conducting a search and rescue mission. POB: 4, Injuries: None, Damage: Unknown. 9/24/2025 0603Z.

ALBANY, OR, (S12), (FSOC FSS), (S12), N20490, C172. N20490 (CVO-S12) experienced a hard landing and bounced causing damage to the propeller, firewall and fuselage. FAA Form 8020-9 was received. POB: 1, Injuries: None, Damage: Unknown. Late Report: 8/18/2025 2045Z.

N707AT (VFR) requested an inbound full stop from the northeast (NE). The pilot 4 NM NE of the airfield reported a rough-running engine, and at 2 NM the pilot declared an emergency for engine outage, proceeding directly to the runway environment. At approximately 1.5 NM NE of the runway, the pilot declared they were not going to make it to the runway and turned northbound to land in a field. The pilot reported landing safely with no damage/injuries to report.

N999AD departed COS and was told to resume its own altitude and navigation. 1 min later, Mayday was broadcast over the frequency. ATC repeated tries for who was calling the Mayday were not answered. There was lost radar with N999AD. The aircraft was reported to have landed on the side of the road approximately 5 miles north of COS. The only injury was to the pilot and was reported as minor.

SPTN622 departed from the TXY A/E intersection, which was right in front of the nonmovement area ramp where the Snowbird flight (SNBD) had just parked. During liftoff, SPTN622 lifted off, causing FOD to possibly cause damage to the SNBD aircraft.
